items.title: Taiwan opposition leader says US trip 'exceeded expectations'
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan's main opposition leader said her tour of the United States aimed at mustering support for her stance on China "exceeded expectations", without detailing any tangible outcomes. Kuomintang (KMT) chairwoman Cheng Li-wun returned to Taipei this week to sharp questions over the island's defence spending after her party thwarted the Taiwanese government's plan to spend nearly US$40 billion on critical weapons, including US arms and domestically produced drones. Cheng's reception in the US was "lukewarm", said Wen-Ti Sung, a political analyst from the Atlantic Council's Global China Hub.

items.title: Man held after alleged assault, threat with object resembling pistol in Cheras
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a man in connection with an assault and criminal intimidation case involving a victim who was allegedly threatened with an object resembling a firearm at an eatery in Desa Sri Puteri here. Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the incident occurred at about 11.05am on June 9 at a restaurant. "Police have classified the case under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt, Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation, and Section 8 of the Firearms Act 1960," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Former tenant arrested over vandalism at Bukit Jalil residence guardhouse
items.summary: Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the suspect was detained by the district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department (D7) team at the Brickfields district police headquarters on June 14. "Initial investigations revealed that the suspect, a former tenant, allegedly became enraged and damaged the guardhouse on April 23 after becoming dissatisfied with the management. Rosdi said the suspect admitted his involvement in the incident during questioning.

items.title: Perak Perhilitan relocates herd of 12 wild elephants from Kampung Bongor to forest reserve
items.summary: GERIK, June 17 — The Perak Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) has managed to relocate a herd of 12 wild elephants in the area of Kampung Bongor in a recent operation. Its director Mohd Taufik Abdul Rahman said that the elephants were moved in stages from the village since Monday with the help of two female elephants Indah and Suria and will be relocated to their natural habitat far from human settlements. “All adult and young elephants found were estimated to be about one and a half years to 35 years old,” he said when contacted by Bernama, adding that the department received complaints from villagers about the presence of elephants in their area recently.

items.title: UK PM says Russian warship's warning shots 'reckless' not 'sinister'
items.summary: LONDON: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Wednesday said a Russian warship's warning shots fired near a yacht in the English Channel were "reckless" but not "sinister", in the latest at-sea tension between London and Moscow. Moscow said earlier the frigate fired the shots after the UK-registered yacht made a "dangerous approach." "Following attempts to contact a British vessel in the channel, the Grigorovich fired warning shots.

items.title: Four measures to ensure long-term labour market resilience
items.summary: The data indicate that in 2022, 391,900 out of 549,100 unemployed Malaysians (71.4 per cent) were Bumiputera. In 2023, the figure stood at 354,900 out of 483,700 unemployed Malaysian citizens (73.4 per cent). In 2024, 336,300 out of 463,900 unemployed Malaysian citizens (72.5 per cent) were Bumiputera.

items.title: Relief centre closes after all 110 Kota Baru fire victims find homes
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A temporary relief centre at Masjid Sultan Ismail Petra Mukim Banggol, which housed victims of the Kampung Gok Kapur fire, has closed after all affected families were provided with alternative accommodation. Kelantan Welfare Department director Che Samsuzuki Che Noh said the centre ceased operations at 5pm on June 12 after all 27 affected households, comprising 110 people, left the facility. Che Samsuzuki said the rental homes were secured through coordination between local authorities and the affected families, with rental assistance provided by the state government for up to three months.

items.title: Maintenance worker, 24, drowns during water tower tank repair in Bandar Saujana Perdana
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre chief Hamdan Hamzah said the 24-year-old victim was found unconscious on top of the tower reservoir tank before being pronounced dead at the scene. “The fire engine arrived at the scene at 12.17pm and found the victim unconscious. Hamdan said firefighters subsequently retrieved and brought the victim down from the tank.

items.title: MP slams Prisons Dept for dodging serious allegations in Taiping inmate death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A member of parliament has criticised the Prisons Department for failing to respond to findings by the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) on the Taiping Prison incident, which resulted in the death of an inmate. "The real issue is not merely that one officer has been charged or that five personnel are facing disciplinary action. On May 25, Suhakam found that Prisons Department personnel committed constitutional and human rights violations during a transfer operation at Taiping Prison.

items.title: Amanah Raya, JPPH launch digital platform to accelerate estate administration process
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amanah Raya Bhd and the Property Valuation and Services Department (JPPH) have entered into a strategic partnership through the introduction of "JPPH Drive", an integrated digital platform aimed at accelerating and streamlining the estate administration process. In a statement, AmanahRaya said the latest digital initiative is designed to enhance the efficiency of property valuation services by reducing dependence on manual processes previously used in estate management. AmanahRaya acting group managing director Jack Yap Ngee Heong said the collaboration reflects the company's commitment to improving customer experience through innovative and more efficient service solutions.

items.title: Frameworks alone not enough, Anwar urges businesses and innovators to drive Asean‑Russia cooperation
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Finalisation of the Asean-Russia Strategic Programme on Trade and Investment Cooperation 2026-2035 is a shot in the arm for the next leap in economic ties, but an enabling environment is key to turning them into reality, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. The programme, set to be adopted later this year, provides the roadmap for the next phase of economic relations between the 11-member bloc and Russia, focusing on trade and investment, emerging technologies, and climate resilience. “Economic resilience should complement international cooperation, not replace it,” he said.

items.title: #NSTViral: Clip showing alleged family dispute at fast-food outlet sparks concern online
items.summary: KULAI: A viral video allegedly showing a heated altercation involving a married couple at a fast-food restaurant near Senai International Airport has drawn concern from social media users, with many urging relevant parties to look into the matter. The incident gained attention after a Threads user shared a post claiming to have witnessed the confrontation. The user alleged that the man shouted at the woman and appeared to threaten violence.

items.title: Nanta confirms he will remain politically active, laughs off speculation on next move
items.summary: KUCHING, June 17 — Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) secretary-general Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi remained tight-lipped on Wednesday over whether he would defend his parliamentary seat or transition to state politics. Pressed about his political future amid ongoing speculation over candidate negotiations for the coming Sarawak election, Nanta said the matter had already received extensive media coverage. Nanta, who is also Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB) secretary-general and Kapit MP, indicated that there were plans for his next political move but said he was not ready to disclose further details.

items.title: Berjasa aims to contest 15 seats in Negri Sembilan election
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Berjaya hopes to contest 15 state seats in the Negri Sembilan election, with a focus on suburban and rural constituencies that have sizeable Malay and young voter populations. Its president, Zamani Ibrahim, said the proposed seat allocation had yet to be finalised as the party would first hold further discussions with its ally, Pas. "For the Negri Sembilan election, we intend to contest 15 state seats.

items.title: US holds off blacklisting China's DeepSeek, over 100 firms deemed security risks
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US has held off adding China's AI startup DeepSeek, memory chipmaker CXMT and more than 100 other companies flagged as national security risks to a trade blacklist, according to two people familiar with the matter, as the Trump administration tries to avoid escalating tensions with Beijing. DeepSeek, CXMT and other companies were approved by an interagency committee last year for addition to the Commerce Department's Entity List, which is being reported for the first time. Reuters is also exclusively reporting the large number of companies awaiting publication on the list.

items.title: Pas-Bersatu rift could cost PN GE16
items.summary: "This was decided by Pas, not Bersatu. Pas has effectively removed Bersatu from their cooperation. So how can Pas still use the PN coalition when Bersatu is part of it," he asked.

items.title: MACC to probe governance weaknesses in Daya Kerjaya 2.0 scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will examine governance and procedural weaknesses in the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 employment incentive programme as part of its ongoing investigation into alleged fraudulent claims amounting to RM9 million. He said MACC, through its Governance Investigation Division, would intensify efforts to assist government departments, agencies and companies in improving governance practices and tightening procedures to prevent leakages or abuse involving public funds. Last week, MACC remanded 73 individuals , including agents and company owners, suspected of submitting fraudulent claims amounting to about RM9 million under Daya Kerjaya 2.0.

items.title: Indonesia prepares maiden panda bond sale in China
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa will meet with Chinese investors and his Chinese counterpart, Lan Fo'an, during a visit to Beijing this week, with a sale of yuan-denominated bonds at the top of his agenda. Here are some details of his trip: The main agenda for the trip involves the preparation for Indonesia's sale in mainland China of yuan-denominated bonds, known as panda bonds, according to a statement from the finance ministry on Wednesday. Purbaya's trip aims to broaden Indonesia's bond investor base, the finance ministry said.

items.title: Mother and 11-year-old son killed in head-on collision near Kulai
items.summary: KULAI: A woman and her 11-year-old son were killed in a head-on collision involving a four-wheel-drive vehicle and a lorry on Jalan Gunung Pulai here last night. The 36-year-old woman was driving the four-wheel-drive vehicle with four family members as passengers during the 8pm crash. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Tan Seng Lee said preliminary investigations revealed that the woman's vehicle is believed to have entered the opposite lane before crashing into the oncoming lorry.

items.title: IEA sees gradual Hormuz recovery tipping into significant 2027 surplus
items.summary: LONDON: The world oil market will recover gradually from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz before tipping into a significant surplus in 2027, the International Energy Agency said in its monthly oil market report on Wednesday. The US and Iran reached an agreement to end the three-month-old war, which includes Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz and the US lifting its naval blockade, potentially bringing an end to the largest oil supply disruption in history which shut in over 14 million barrels per day of Middle East oil output, according to the IEA. The oil market will then enter a significant supply overhang next year, the IEA said in its first look at 2027, with global oil supply set to surge by 8 million barrels per day and demand rising by just 2 million barrels per day.

items.title: Grenade exercise tragedy: Siti Khadijah wanted to follow in father's footsteps
items.summary: KLUANG: A retired soldier's dream of seeing his youngest daughter continuing the family's military legacy ended in tragedy when Private Siti Khadijah Sungip, 24, was killed during a grenade exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, yesterday. The victim's elder sister, Siti Zaiha, 39, said Siti Khadijah sent a photograph via WhatsApp at midnight on June 15, which is now the family's last keepsake. "Siti Khadijah said she had more training the next day and that the grenade exercise would start around 8am.

items.title: Brexit 10 years on: UK exporters still adjusting to life outside the EU
items.summary: TELFORD: When Brexit abruptly ended British cheesemaker Michael Harte's ambitious European export plans, four years passed before his young company Bridge Cheese found overseas customers for its cheddar and mozzarella products again - in faraway Hong Kong. Harte had banked on a "soft Brexit" that kept frictionless trade after Britain's shock June 23, 2016 decision to leave the European Union, he said. "We just weren't competitive," said Harte.

items.title: Kelantan police say petrol smuggling at Malaysia‑Thailand border remains low, controlled despite West Asia tensions
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — Petrol smuggling activities along the Malaysia-Thailand border, particularly in Kelantan, remain at a low and controlled level despite concerns over the impact of ongoing tensions in West Asia on regional fuel supply. According to him, the situation remains manageable due to proactive and coordinated enforcement measures conducted through large-scale integrated operations at border entry points and petrol stations within identified risk zones. Mohd Yusoff said daily control operations and patrols conducted by the General Operations Force (GOF), together with police personnel from nearby stations, would continue to be intensified at identified petrol stations to prevent any leakage of subsidised fuel.

items.title: Norway crown princess has successful lung transplant
items.summary: OSLO: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffers from a rare form of pulmonary fibrosis that causes breathing difficulties, has undergone a "successful" lung transplant, the palace said on Wednesday. In accordance with standard practice for all recent transplant recipients, Her Royal Highness the Crown Princess will remain admitted to Oslo University Hospital Rikshospitalet for several weeks to come," Are Holm, lung specialist at the hospital, said in the palace statement. Doctors said the procedure was a last resort when a patient is believed to have less than two years to live without new lungs.

items.title: Indian firms invest over US3bil in Malaysia, create 30,000 jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Indian companies have invested more than US$3 billion (US$1 = RM4.06) in Malaysia and created over 30,000 direct jobs, reflecting the strong economic partnership between Malaysia and India, according to the Consortium of Indian Industries in Malaysia (CIIM). CIIM chairman Datuk Umang Sharma said the investments had contributed significantly to Malaysia's economic development and underscored the growing role of Indian businesses in the country. Reddy, who recently completed his tenure as Indian High Commissioner to Malaysia.

items.title: DeepSeek valued at more than US$50bil after funding round: reports
items.summary: BEIJING: Investors have valued Chinese artificial intelligence startup DeepSeek at more than US$50 billion in the company's first fundraising round, reports said. That values the company at more than US$50 billion, they said. US startup Anthropic is valued at US$965 billion following a US$65 billion fundraising round, while ChatGPT-maker OpenAI was valued at US$852 billion in March.

items.title: G7 leaders to discuss global economic recovery
items.summary: EVIAN: Leaders of the Group of Seven (G7) industrialised democracies will discuss ways to support sustainable economic growth on Wednesday as they grapple with the strain the war in Iran is placing on the global economy, reported German news agency dpa. France holds the group's rotating presidency this year. US President Donald Trump's aggressive tariff policies have caused unease among Washington's G7 partners and are expected to feature prominently in discussions.

items.title: Sisters and their husbands rearrested after 2 more former maids file abuse reports
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Two married couples previously remanded over a viral maid abuse video were rearrested today after two more former domestic helpers lodged police reports. Indonesian Embassy counsel Mohd Daud Ismail said magistrate Chai Ing Hien granted a four-day remand order against all suspects today for investigations involving the two victims under the Johor Baru Selatan police jurisdiction. On June 14, Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the two couples were initially arrested after a video showing a foreign domestic worker being assaulted by two men and two women at a house in Taman Johor, Tampoi, went viral.

items.title: Claims of 'backdoor' entry into public universities could erode public trust - Zambry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The allegation that between 30,000 and 60,000 students gained admission to public universities through the 'backdoor' is a serious accusation that could undermine public confidence in the integrity of the country's higher education system. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the baseless claim not only tarnishes the reputation of public universities but also creates the perception that opportunities meant for qualified students had been taken away for financial gain. He said such a narrative could erode public trust, particularly among parents and students currently undergoing the admission process to higher education institutions.

items.title: MACC taps into creative arts to spread anti-corruption message at Youth Film Festival
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is strengthening efforts to cultivate integrity and raise anti-corruption awareness among young people through a strategic collaboration in the organisation of the 5th Youth Film Festival (FFAM) at Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) in Penang. The festival, held on June 14 under the theme "Corruption", served as a platform for university students to express their ideas and perspectives on corruption, integrity and abuse of power through the production of creative, critical and impactful short films. MACC Community Education Division deputy director Samsul Salip said the commission viewed art and visual storytelling as effective mediums for delivering messages on integrity and anti-corruption to society, particularly the younger generation.

items.title: Singapore workers among world's most active AI users: report
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Employees in Singapore are among the world's most active and responsible users of artificial intelligence (AI), Microsoft's 2026 Work Trend Index showed Tuesday, reported Xinhua. Microsoft said in a statement that its study found that 66 per cent of AI users in Singapore are producing work they could not have created a year ago, exceeding the global average of 58 per cent. Overall, Singapore ranked second globally on Microsoft's AI Diffusion Index, reflecting widespread adoption of AI across the workforce.

items.title: Hobart Camp grenade blast kills soldier’s daughter Siti Khadijah, who dreamed of following father’s legacy
items.summary: KLUANG, June 17 — A retired soldier’s dream of seeing his youngest daughter continuing the family’s military legacy ended in tragedy when Private Siti Khadijah Sungip, 24, was killed during a grenade exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, yesterday. The victim’s elder sister, Siti Zaiha, 39, said Siti Khadijah sent a photograph via WhatsApp at midnight on June 15, which is now the family’s last keepsake. “Siti Khadijah said she had more training the next day and that the grenade exercise would start around 8am.

items.title: MIDP: People debt threatens performance as firms face AI-era challenges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As businesses grapple with geopolitical uncertainty, rapid advances in artificial intelligence and shifting workforce expectations, employee wellbeing is increasingly being recognised as a key driver of corporate performance rather than a competing priority. "One of the biggest misconceptions in business is that high performance and employee wellbeing sit at opposite ends of the spectrum. The programme will feature nine sessions aimed at equipping executives, human resources practitioners and organisational leaders with practical frameworks to strengthen workplace culture, employee engagement and organisational performance.

items.title: Minister warns false claims of 60,000 backdoor entries risk eroding trust in higher education
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The allegation that between 30,000 and 60,000 students gained admission to public universities (UA) through the ‘backdoor’ is a serious accusation that could undermine public confidence in the integrity of the country’s higher education system. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the baseless claim not only tarnishes the reputation of public universities but also creates the perception that opportunities meant for qualified students had been taken away for financial gain. He said such a narrative could erode public trust, particularly among parents and students currently undergoing the admission process to higher education institutions (IPTs).

items.title: 62 foreigners nabbed in Selangor immigration raids for pass abuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 62 foreigners in a special enforcement operation targeting the abuse of immigration passes and violations of pass conditions at several locations in Selangor. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the operation, conducted on June 15, covered Cyberjaya, Bandar Seri Putra, Bandar Teknologi Kajang, Seri Kembangan, Sepang and Dengkil. "A total of 62 foreigners were detained for suspected offences under the Immigration Act and Immigration Regulations," Zakaria said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia, Tatarstan see vast potential to expand cooperation in various fields
items.summary: KAZAN: Malaysia and the Republic of Tatarstan have vast potential to expand cooperation in various fields, including trade, investment, education, tourism, the halal industry, technology and talent development, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said the energy sector was among the key areas of discussion, given that Tatarstan is one of the major oil-producing regions in Russia. Anwar said he and Minnikhanov also discussed the role of the Kazan Forum in strengthening business ties while creating new opportunities in innovation, the digital economy and strategic investments.

items.title: Telegram challenging Indian government order that temporarily blocks its use
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Messaging app Telegram has filed a petition in court challenging an Indian government order that has temporarily blocked use of the platform in an effort to prevent exam fraud, legal website Bar and Bench reported on Wednesday. Last month, the Indian government cancelled a key undergraduate entrance exam for medical colleges after authorities said they were investigating allegations that its questions had been leaked. The move, which is unprecedented in India, was "in response to the organised use of the platform by cheating rackets to defraud candidates appearing for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test 2026 re-examination scheduled on 21 June 2026," the Ministry of Education's National Testing Agency said on Tuesday.

items.title: Mass fish kill in Danau Kota: DBKL explains why heavy rain may be to blame
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — City Hall (DBKL) has responded to reports of a mass fish kill at Taman Danau Kota Lake yesterday, confirming that comprehensive clean-up operations were completed on the same day. “DBKL, alongside the Federal Territory’s Department of Environment, also conducted water sampling on June 16 for quality analysis to pinpoint the exact factors contributing to the incident,” DBKL said. DBKL noted that Taman Danau Kota Lake serves a dual purpose: it is a recreational park that also functions as a flood retention pond.

items.title: Protect e‑hailing drivers: Lee Lam Thye calls for dashcams, panic buttons and tougher checks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Protecting e-hailing drivers must be made a national priority amid increasing reports of assault, intimidation, robbery and violence by unruly passengers, said Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye. He said the safety and well-being of e-hailing drivers must therefore be given greater attention by all stakeholders, including the government, e-hailing platform operators, law enforcement agencies and passengers themselves. “Law enforcement agencies must continue to act firmly against individuals who assault, threaten or harass e-hailing drivers.

items.title: Malaysian Army chief orders immediate halt to grenade drills after deadly accident in Kedah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The Malaysian Army has halted all grenade technique exercises with immediate effect following a deadly training incident at Hobart Camp, Kedah, which killed two soldiers yesterday. Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said the suspension is a necessary step to facilitate a comprehensive probe into the incident, NST reported. “We have suspended grenade-throwing training with immediate effect to allow investigations to be carried out,” he said.

items.title: Anwar hails TV AlHijrah’s role in Islamic broadcasting as station turns 16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that broadcasting grounded in values, knowledge and wisdom continues to hold a special place in society despite rapid changes and growing challenges in the media landscape. In a Facebook post marking the 16th anniversary of TV AlHijrah today, he expressed hope that the milestone would spur the station to further progress as a platform for public enlightenment. He said the anniversary should serve as a catalyst for TV AlHijrah to advance its role in promoting moderate Islamic understanding, nurturing human values and supporting the Malaysia MADANI vision.

items.title: No new leads as search for two missing fishermen in Kemaman enters Day 7
items.summary: CHUKAI, June 17 — The search and rescue operation (Carilamat) for two fishermen who are feared missing after their boat sank in Kemaman waters on June 10 continued today, with the search area covering 50.40 square nautical miles. Kemaman Zone Maritime director Abdul Halim Hamzah said the operation, which entered its seventh day, involved 19 members of the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) and eight members of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). He said the assets involved an MMEA ship, KM Redang, with 19 members, and a PDRM boat, PLC 3.

items.title: World Cup 2026: RTM apologises after technical glitch disrupts France-Senegal live stream for Malaysian fans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 – Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) has issued a formal apology to football fans following a technical glitch on its RTMKlik platform that disrupted the live broadcast of the France-Senegal World Cup match earlier today. In a statement today, the national broadcaster attributed the failure to an “unforeseen technical issue.” RTM confirmed that its technical team and technology providers acted immediately to restore the service as quickly as possible. As the official broadcaster of the Fifa World Cup 2026, RTM is tasked with airing all 104 matches of the tournament, including 94 live broadcasts and 10 delayed telecasts.

items.title: IIUM rector Osman Bakar is Malaysia’s Tokoh Maal Hijrah 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 17 — International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM) Rector Prof Emeritus Datuk Dr Osman Bakar was named the National Tokoh Maal Hijrah at the national-level Maal Hijrah 1448H/2026 celebration at Putra Mosque here today. At the same ceremony, renowned Islamic scholar from Morocco, Dr Ahmad Al-Raysuni, was honoured as the International Tokoh Maal Hijrah. This year’s National Maal Hijrah celebration carried the theme “MADANI Dihayati, Ummah Diberkati”, reflecting efforts to strengthen values of compassion, unity and community well-being.

items.title: Works minister says rising construction material costs still manageable, contractors able to absorb increases
items.summary: KUCHING, June 17 — The increase in the cost of construction materials following the global energy crisis and disruptions to the world’s supply chain is still at a manageable level. Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said that for now, contractors are still able to absorb the cost increase and continue with the project without any delays. He said the study included short-term and long-term strategies to deal with any economic impact, especially those involving the supply chain and the cost of construction materials.

items.title: Energy sector in focus as Anwar says Tatarstan’s oil strength offers Malaysia downstream and petrochemical opportunities
items.summary: KAZAN, June 17 — Malaysia and the Republic of Tatarstan have vast potential to expand cooperation in various fields, including trade, investment, education, tourism, the halal industry, technology and talent development, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the energy sector was among the key areas of discussion, given that Tatarstan is one of the major oil-producing regions in Russia. Anwar said he and Minnikhanov also discussed the role of the Kazan Forum in strengthening business ties while creating new opportunities in innovation, the digital economy and strategic investments.

items.title: Villagers discover body of 48‑year‑old man in Kampung Togudon forest, Penampang police say struck by falling tree
items.summary: PENAMPANG, June 17 — A man was found dead inside a forest at Kampung Togudon here on Monday. Penampang district police chief Supt Sammy Newton said villagers discovered the 48-year-old victim lying on the ground at around 6am. “Police investigation revealed that the victim had entered the forest alone on June 10 to check and maintain his bat net.

items.title: Sabah’s Papar district to gain extra 10 million litres daily as water projects near completion, says Armizan
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 17 — Two ongoing water infrastructure construction projects in Papar will be operational this year to enable a long-term solution to the issue of clean water supply disruptions in the district is realised. Papar Member of Parliament Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the commitment was given by the contractor in a special meeting with the Sabah State Water Department (JANS) and related agencies chaired by the Sabah State Secretary (SKN) recently. The Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living said the Kampung Kabang water intake upgrading project was given a commitment to be completed by July 31, while the Kogopon 2 Water Treatment Plant (WTP) component is targeted to be operational in stages from August to September.

items.title: Op Drain: MACC nabs 13 including govt agency director over ‘contract cartel’ scandal
items.summary: IPOH, June 17 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has secured remand orders for 13 individuals, including a current and former director of a government agency, following allegations of a RM2.5 million bribery scheme involving public contracts. Three suspects, including two civil servants and a company director, will remain in custody until today. The remaining 10 suspects face a five-day remand expiring on June 20.

items.title: No more ‘green wave’?: PAS sounds the alarm over parties going after the 40pc youth vote
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — PAS is concerned with the emergence of new political parties specifically designed to attract young voters ahead of the next general election, describing the trend as a significant strategic challenge. PAS vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah noted that these new entities appear to have meticulously mapped out their target demographics, focusing on youth who remain unaligned with any existing political party, NST reported. “What they say can easily influence the minds of young voters.

items.title: Diarrhoea, vomiting claims spark probe at Janda Baik river, Pahang Health Dept steps up checks
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 17 — The Pahang State Health Department (JKNP) is conducting an investigation following allegations that several visitors experienced symptoms of diarrhoea, vomiting and fever after bathing in a river at a recreational area near Bentong recently. JKNP in a statement announced that a risk assessment had been carried out and the results of the initial investigation found that there were no notifications of food poisoning incidents or an unusual increase in acute gastroenteritis (AGE) cases received through the existing surveillance system. JKNP announced that control and prevention actions being implemented include active case detection, epidemiological investigations to identify sources of exposure and related risk factors as well as environmental risk assessments in the recreational areas involved.

items.title: KL police use viral clip to track down teen over bike stunts from May (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — A viral video of a motorcyclist performing stunts on the streets of the capital has led to an arrest, after the city’s Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) tracked down the offender. The investigation began after JSPT flagged a 1 minute 23 second clip uploaded to Facebook by account "Kyle Martopo." Police have since confirmed that the incident took place on May 17, at approximately 3.10am along Jalan Tun Razak.

items.title: Malaysia acquires RM423.8m ANKA‑S drones, capable of 24‑hour flights at 30,000 feet
items.summary: LABUAN, June 17 — The operation of the Royal Malaysian Air Force’s (RMAF) ANKA-S Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) marks a strategic leap in strengthening the nation’s defence and maritime surveillance capabilities, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said the asset is capable of providing operational coverage previously unavailable, particularly in strategic maritime areas including the South China Sea, giving Malaysia a critical information advantage in safeguarding national security and sovereignty. “The operation of the ANKA-S aircraft represents a strategic leap in terms of our country’s defence and maritime surveillance.

items.title: Here comes the boom: Malaysia inks deal for France’s Caesar mobile artillery and local tech transfer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Malaysia has significantly upgraded its land-based firepower, securing 18 Caesar artillery systems in a deal announced yesterday at the Eurosatory 2026 international defence and security exhibition. Representatives from KNDS and local industrial partner Advanced Defense System (ADS Sdn Bhd) inked the deal on Tuesday. In 2018, KNDS and Malaysian authorities reached a similar milestone with the local assembly of 105LG lightweight towed artillery guns.

items.title: Police bust student courier with RM1.17m ganja haul at Bukit Kayu Hitam
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 17 — Police have seized 14.63 kilogrammes (kg) of ganja buds worth an estimated RM1.17 million from a car at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex. Kedah deputy police chief DCP Baderulhisham Baharudin said the 24-year-old Malaysian driver of the car, who is also a student at a private higher education institution in Kuala Lumpur, was arrested during the operation on Monday. “The total value of the drug seizure is RM1.17 million.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of intense storms across peninsula until noon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has triggered a thunderstorm alert for a wide stretch of Peninsular Malaysia this morning. Residents are urged to prepare for heavy rain and strong winds, with the warning remaining active until 12:00pm today. In Perak, the affected districts are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim.

items.title: PM Anwar, Uzbekistan president discuss energy, trade, halal industry and tourism during Tashkent transit stop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 17 — Malaysia and Uzbekistan have the potential to expand cooperation in various strategic sectors, including energy, trade, investment, education, the halal industry and tourism, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. According to him, among the sectors discussed were trade, investment, education, the halal industry and tourism. Anwar said both countries also saw ample room to strengthen cooperation in the energy sector, especially the gas and petroleum industries.

items.title: Army corporal Norazmi laid to rest in Merbok with full military honours after Gurun grenade blast
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 17 — The remains of the army personnel who died in an explosion while undergoing training at Hobart Camp, Gurun were buried at Al Busyra Mosque Muslim Cemetery, Merbok near here at 5.25am today according to military ceremonies. After that, the remains were taken to the Al Busyra Mosque, Merbok for prayers led by the Religious Staff Officer of the Sixth Malaysian Infantry Brigade, Major Mohd Sobri Che Hassan. The deceased Norazmi left behind two children, Nurliana Danishah, 14, and Muhammad Rizhan Darwish, 8.

items.title: Smuggling smashed in Tumpat jetty raid as GOF seizes RM1.84m Thai batik haul
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 17 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade seized 8,000 pieces of smuggled batik cloth from Thailand and a lorry in an operation in Kampung Simpangan, Tumpat, yesterday. “Several men were seen transferring the sacks into the lorry before the lorry moved away in a suspicious manner. The inspection found that the sacks contained 8,800 pieces of batik cloth believed to have been smuggled in from Thailand, he said, adding that a man, 32, was arrested and the cloth (worth RM1.76 million) and the lorry (worth RM80,000) totalling RM1.84 million were seized.

items.title: Police arrest four in Lahad Datu following gang rape of teenager in her home
items.summary: The matter came to light after the victim’s family members became aware of the incident, leading to a formal police report, Sinar Harian reported. Lahad Datu district police chief ACP Dzulbaharin Ismail said that the incident occurred while the victim was sleeping alone in her bedroom. “The victim only lodged a police report after the incident became known to her family,” Dzulbaharin said in a statement last night.
